name: Muhammad Husnain
role: Computer Science Student · Developer · Automation Specialist · Digital Solutions Architect
heroTitle: I build premium digital systems for modern businesses.
heroSubtitle: Computer Science thinking, polished interfaces, ecommerce execution, automation workflows, and practical digital solutions—designed to help businesses operate better and grow with clarity.
education: Bachelor’s in Computer Science · University of Engineering and Technology
aboutShort: I am a Computer Science student and multi-disciplinary technical professional who enjoys turning complex business problems into clear, reliable digital systems.
aboutLong: My journey began with programming and web development, then expanded into ecommerce, software systems, digital advertising, and workflow automation. That combination helps me see more than a single screen or feature—I look at the complete user and business journey.

I care about clean engineering, thoughtful design, understandable communication, and practical outcomes. Whether I am building a Shopify experience, a React interface, an automation workflow, or a technical prototype, the goal is the same: create something useful, maintainable, and ready for real people.

I am currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science at the University of Engineering and Technology, strengthening my foundations in programming, data structures, algorithms, system design, and technical problem solving while continuing to deliver practical digital work.

principles.title: Think in systems
principles.text: Connect design, code, data, operations, and growth instead of treating them as isolated tasks.

principles.title: Make complexity clear
principles.text: Turn technical requirements into understandable experiences and manageable solutions.

principles.title: Build for change
principles.text: Use modular structures so content, features, and projects can evolve without redesigning everything.

principles.title: Stay honest
principles.text: Avoid inflated claims, communicate limitations, and focus on work that can be demonstrated.
